show.headline: Come watch some silly cat videos for a good cause

event.title: <event-type-title>Alamo Crafthouse:</event-type-title> <show-title>CatVideoFest 2026</show-title>
event.headline: Watch cat videos, paint your own cat lamp
event.description: It’s true what they say: cat people are craft people. At our Crafthouse screening, a live host (almost certainly covered in cat hair) will guide you through painting your own cat lamp while CATVIDEOFEST 2026 plays on the big screen. Craft from your seat with the lights up, then take your masterpiece home. No litter box required.

eventType.title: Alamo Crafthouse
eventType.headline: Enjoy your favorite movies with a crafting activity tailored for the film
eventType.description: At Alamo Crafthouse, we're bringing your favorite movies with a bit of extra fun. Each event includes an activity tailored specifically for the film, from crocheting to calligraphy, and the lights will stay on so you can craft along to the movie. Let's sip, snack, and craft!

show.title: The Phantom of the Opera (2004)
show.headline: The classic musical comes to life on the big screen

event.title: <event-type-title>Alamo Crafthouse:</event-type-title> <show-title>The Phantom of the Opera (2004)</show-title>
event.headline: Craft in the shadows of a classic romance
event.description: How’s this for a strange duet? Joel Schumacher’s 2004 gothic romance paired with hands-on crafting designed just for the movie. Each guest receives two craft sets to work on from their seat, including Phantom-inspired calligraphy with guided activity sheets and a card to fill in, sign, and seal. You’ll also create an iconic red paper rose with step-by-step instructions, all while enjoying food and drinks delivered right to your seat.

titleCardImage.altText: Event Category Web Hero3 Fan Event 1920x1080

show.title: 13 Going on 30
show.headline: For some, 13 feels like it was just yesterday. For Jenna, it was.

event.title: <show-title>13 Going on 30</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: Celebrate being 30, flirty & thriving at our interactive movie party
event.description:
When 13 GOING ON 30 was released over 20 years ago(!), I was overjoyed at the fact that Hollywood had finally decided to make BIG for girls. Don't get me wrong, it was awesome to watch Tom Hanks spend his paycheck on video games and a penthouse with a trampoline, but now it was a gal's turn to live the dream, and Jennifer Garner did not disappoint. This film features the greatest fantasies of a 13-year-old girl*: a makeover montage in a gigantic closet, a high-power job working at a fashion magazine, a massive slumber party, and, of course, a totally cute guy.

But 13 GOING ON 30 isn't just a fun premise, it's a heartfelt and hilarious coming-of-age story. The cast is top-notch, with Garner perfectly embodying her inner teen, Judy Greer serving up some major sass (like she does), and Mark Ruffalo being as foxy and charming as he's ever been. This delightful cinematic concoction is better than a pack of Razzles, so join us and experience the thrill of being 30, flirty, and thriving.

*Or any girl, regardless of her age. Because that shoe collection! WANT. (Sarah Pitre)

Your ticket includes the following props:

  • Butterfly tattoo – Just like Jenna's favorite accessory.
  • Tissues – No need to look like a model. You're beautiful just as you are.
  • Mantra sleepmask – The best years of your life.
  • Wishing dust - What do you wish for in your heart of hearts?

show.title: Elf
show.headline: The best way to spread Christmas cheer

event.title: <show-title>Elf</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: A cheerful celebration with themed games, props & singing loud for all to hear
event.description:
"Buddy the Elf, what's your favorite color?"

Don’t be a cotton-headed ninnymuggins this holiday season. Come out to Alamo Drafthouse and spread the cheer at this sugar-fueled, interactive holiday spectacular, packed with games, props, and, of course, singing loud for all to hear.

Bundle up for this celebration of one of the best holiday films ever made, period. Will Ferrell’s take on a North Pole elf trying to figure out Manhattan (“Watch out! The yellow ones don’t stop!”) just might be the funniest he’s ever been.

Our ELF Movie Party features all sorts of fun props, a gift "wrap off" before the film, and of course, your very own elf hat.

contentBlocks.paragraphs: Whether it’s maple syrup or another refreshing beverage, it’ll go down better in our exclusive ELF 16 oz. collector’s glass. Featuring the movie logo on one side and the phrase “Cotton-headed Ninny-muggins” on the other, this pint glass is available for a limited time on AlamoMart.com. Order now and proudly celebrate Christmas in July at the ELF Movie Party.

show.title: The Fast and the Furious: 25th Anniversary
show.headline: Start your engines

event.title: <show-title>The Fast and the Furious: 25th Anniversary</show-title> <event-type-title>Movie Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: A NOS-fueled party for family
event.description: Join us for a high-octane movie party celebrating the 25th anniversary of the original FAST movie. Before the cars in Tokyo drifted, before they went to space, and before any Vin Diesel feuds, it was just a simple undercover cop story about stolen VCRs, street racing, and loyalty tested at 100 miles per hour. Strap in for this special screening with a live host, exclusive themed keepsakes like a bumper magnet, bottle opener, and a NOS keychain, as well as a theater full of family.

event.title: <show-title>Six: The Musical Live!</show-title> <event-type-title>Sing-Along</event-type-title>
event.headline: Belt out the hits from the West End smash
event.description:
Get ready to lose your head at this special sing-along screening of SIX: THE MUSICAL LIVE! This is your chance to rule the room alongside the queens and sing along with every iconic number in a theater full of fellow Tudorphiles.

Please note that our usual No Talking/No Singing Showtunes policy is relaxed for this screening, but please be reasonable and save a little vocal power for the rest of the monarchy.

show.title: Wet Hot American Summer: 25th Anniversary
show.headline: Celebrate 25 years of makeout sessions, talking cans, and trips to town

event.title: <show-title>Wet Hot American Summer: 25th Anniversary</show-title> <event-type-title>Party</event-type-title>
event.headline: Celebrate 25 years of makeout sessions, talking cans, and trips to town
event.description:
They promised to reunite 10 years later, at 9:30, because McKinley had something at 11 and he’d already moved it twice. Now it’s 25 years later, and Camp Firewood is back in session and on the big screen. Exclusively at Alamo, we’re pairing this special anniversary party screening with a themed menu that promises more than canned vegetables, a custom camp T-shirt, and other surprises. Whether you’ve spent the last 25 years wondering if you taste like burger or just trying to make it to the campfire before somebody breaks into song, this is your chance to come back to Camp Firewood. We want you inside us.

All of our campers must adhere to a dress code, so each and every ticket includes a limited edition WET HOT AMERICAN SUMMER 25th Anniversary T-Shirt.

show.title: Tiny Furniture
show.headline: Lena Dunham explores an authentic portrait of a young woman at a crossroads

event.title: <show-title>Tiny Furniture</show-title>
event.headline: Lena Dunham’s awkwardly charming debut reflects her longstanding career
event.description:
Although a polarizing figure, Lena Dunham has created some of the most quintessential millennial coming-of-age films of the 2010s. Her breakout, oddball SXSW indie TINY FURNITURE was the blueprint for her hit TV series Girls, and also starred Jemima Kirke and Alex Karpovsky.

In the film, Dunham plays a version of herself: Aura, an aspiring film graduate who returns to live with her mom and sister in New York after feeling the independence of college. Aura is ready for her career to begin, but doesn’t know where to begin, and takes up a restaurant gig to make money while she sorts out her goals.

Dunham’s projects have always felt personal, but there’s a certain rawness to TINY FURNITURE that’s uncomfortably disturbing but almost impossible to look away from the trainwreck. In the wake of her new memoir Famesick, it’s nostalgic to go back to see where it all began.

show.title: Divine Hammer
show.headline: Usher in a new era of death

event.title: <show-title>Divine Hammer</show-title>
event.headline: Usher in a new era of death
event.description:
Two maladapted young women, both part of the same internet gore enthusiast community, formulate a plan to meet in person with the intent that one will kill the other, film the killing, and then sell the cameras that “watched” to unsuspecting customers online in the hopes of spurring on a “new era of death.”

Inspired in equal parts by mumblecore, shot-on-video horror, and a deep unease formed by the internet’s mass syndication of violence, DIVINE HAMMER is the debut feature by the M. Sisters, the multimedia duo of Hazel and Mae M.

show.title: Eyes Without A Face
show.headline: An elegant and terrifying masterpiece.

event.title: <show-title>Eyes Without A Face</show-title>
event.headline: 4K restoration of the elegant French nightmare.
event.description:
Once seen, this movie is never forgotten. At a secluded chateau, an obsessive doctor attempts a radical plastic surgery procedure to restore his daughter’s disfigured face. But it comes with a horrifying price. Stylishly directed by French cinema giant Georges Franju, EYES WITHOUT A FACE is a ghastly amalgam of mad science and fairy-tale that envelops you like a dreamy fog. The film’s images of terror, gore, and inexplicable beauty led legendary critic Pauline Kael to call it “the most elegant horror movie ever made."

4K restoration courtesy of Janus Films

show.title: The Faculty
show.headline: What if your teachers really were from another planet?

event.title: <show-title>The Faculty</show-title>
event.headline: THE BREAKFAST CLUB meets INVASION OF THE BODY SNATCHERS
event.description: What do you get when you combine the distinct visual eye of Robert Rodriguez with the wit of SCREAM scribe Kevin Williamson? You get THE FACULTY, a late '90s, fun-as-hell entry in sci-fi canon that sees a group of teenagers team up against their body-snatched teachers when aliens decide to land at the doorstep of their high school. Featuring an all-star cast of fresh-faced actors (Elijah Wood, Josh Hartnett, Jordana Brewster, Clea DuVall) as the students and seasoned vets (Bebe Neuwirth, Famke Janssen, Robert Patrick, Piper Laurie) as the titular academics, THE FACULTY is a merging of talents so perfect that no '90s audience could have possibly been prepared. So grab a nice, tall glass of water and get ready for a movie that's guaranteed to jack you up! (Trace Thurman)

show.title: Buffet Infinity
show.headline: A found footage cosmic gumbo

event.title: <show-title>Buffet Infinity</show-title> <event-type-title>+ MAN EATING P***Y Double Feature</event-type-title>
event.headline: A double feature of oddball comedies
event.description:
About BUFFET INFINITY
2026 / 99 mins.

Echoing the Canadian comedy classic SCTV and picking from hundreds of hours of original, low-budget TV ads to tell the sinister tale of two restaurants battling it out in the fictional town of Westridge County. Ads for insurance, used car rivals, a local religious scholar, and a recording artist converge to tell the story of an expanding sinkhole, a cult, and an ever-growing restaurant that becomes unsettlingly sentient.

About MAN EATING P****Y
2026 / 15 mins

A terminally ill man finds solace with an enigmatic prostitute whose inhuman body provides both carnal satisfaction and final deliverance.

titleCardImage.altText: Event Category Web Hero Special Event 1920x1080

show.title: Communion
show.headline: One of the wildest performances in the Christopher Walken canon!

event.title: <show-title>Communion</show-title>
event.headline: One of the wildest performances in the Christopher Walken canon!
event.description:
“Even before the anal probing, he's nuts. From his first breath, he's out of his mind. His approach appears to be mostly ad-libbing. It's a combination of the Goldblum cadence with a touch of Shatner's Shakespearean stagger.” - Scumbalina, Letterboxd

One of the wildest performances in the Christopher Walken canon, and that’s really saying something! COMMUNION director Phillipe Mora (THE HOWLING II and III) explodes UFO guru Whitley Strieber’s foundational text into a watchable SNL parody-type version, perhaps unintentionally…but does it really matter? Here, Walken struts, dances and makes a mockery of our plane of existence and IT’S BEAUTIFUL. You will never, ever get the image of him beaming and cavorting with the little grey aliens out of your mind

DCP courtesy of Shout! Factory and the American Genre Film Archive

event.title: <show-title>Marcel the Shell with Shoes On</show-title>

event.description: This show is part of our Kids Camp series – our summer program dedicated to providing affordable family movies for maximum family fun. Discounted tickets for everyone – little kids, big kids, and grownup kids – making it easy to bring your budding cinephile to the movies.

event.title: <show-title>First Date (2026)</show-title> <event-type-title>+ Whispers Double Feature</event-type-title>
event.headline: Presented by Pickford AI
event.description:
An Alamo Drafthouse Private Event presented by Pickford AI. To book your premiere, visit drafthouse.com/private-events.

Pickford presents an interactive double feature event. Whispers is an AI-powered whodunnit like you've never seen before. Audience members will "whisper" in the ears of Detective Marcus Kent to help crack the case and identify the killer in real-time.

Opening for Whispers is First Date, a comedic interactive experience where the audience creates our dating heroes, coaches what they say, and determines whether romantic sparks fly or ensures they crash and burn.

Audiences set up first dates in this interactive, AI-powered experience

show.title: JEOPARDY! INTERACTIVE
show.headline: America’s Favorite Quiz Show® is headed to the big screen

marketStrings.value: The personal closed captioning device displays feature film dialogue and other audio in the form of English-language text for individuals who are deaf or hard of hearing.

The descriptive audio headset provides individuals who are blind or have low vision with an English-language narration of the details of the feature presentation that can't be understood by the soundtrack alone.

Upon request at the box office, we provide assistive listening headsets with amplified audio for those individuals who are hard of hearing. For guests who prefer to use their own hearing aids, neck loops, or cochlear implants, our assistive listening headsets have a 3.5mm headphone jack to connect to those devices.

In order to ensure the highest quality experience for all guests, you may not enter a movie after it has started. We’d love to see you up to 30 minutes early. Running late? No worries, we will happily exchange your ticket for a different showtime or a raincheck to a future show.

sessionAttributes.name: All Ages
sessionAttributes.description: For all ages shows, we welcome babies and kids of all ages. The no talking rules are relaxed, since kids will be kids. But other than that, you can expect the full Alamo experience.

sessionAttributes.name: Baby Day Show
sessionAttributes.description: For our baby day shows, we keep the lights up a bit, and the sound down a bit, so your little one never has to know they even went to the movies!

sessionAttributes.name: Kid Friendly
sessionAttributes.description: Grab the fam and let the fun begin! Our Kid Friendly screenings include pre-show activity sheets and crayons, a relaxed noise policy, and kids menus. Ages 3+ welcome with a parent or guardian.

agePolicies.description: All minors under the age of 18 must be accompanied by an adult. Children ages 6+ admitted. Children 5 and under (including infants) are ONLY admitted during specifically designated "All Ages" and "Family Friendly" screenings.
